summary: Ukrainian drone attacks killed four civilians in Brovary and injured multiple civilians in Konotop, while Russian forces reported one civilian death from UAV strikes in Belgorod. Ukraine announced the return of 185 soldiers from Russian captivity and a local ceasefire at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant for critical power line repairs. Russian air defenses claimed destruction of 123 Ukrainian UAVs across several regions.
key_points: Four civilians killed in Brovary by Russian drone attack; four injured in Pavlograd and five injured, including three children, in Konotop (Source: @UkraineNow/81689).
One civilian killed and another wounded by Ukrainian drones in Belgorod region (Source: @tass_agency/379467).
Ukraine reports 185 prisoners of war returned from Russian captivity, including personnel from multiple frontlines (Source: @UkraineNow/81705).
Local ceasefire declared at Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant to repair critical transmission line under IAEA supervision (Source: @UkraineNow/81699).
Russian forces claim 123 Ukrainian UAVs destroyed over Belgorod, Bryansk, Voronezh, Kaluga, Kursk, Nizhny Novgorod, Oryol, Tula, Moscow, Crimea, and seas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Two ballistic missiles with cluster warheads struck logistics infrastructure in Odesa region, wounding eight road workers, two seriously (Source: @UkraineNow/81716).
Multiple UAV groups reported moving from Black Sea and regional areas toward Ukrainian targets, including Odessa, Chornomorsk, and Tatarnbari (Source: @kpszsu/64168, @kpszsu/64169).

sections.executive_summary: Four civilians were killed in Brovary by Russian drone strikes, and Ukrainian forces reported a civilian death in Belgorod from their drones. Ukraine announced the return of 185 prisoners of war and a local ceasefire at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant for critical repairs. Russian air defenses claimed to have downed 123 Ukrainian UAVs across multiple regions.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported that Russian drones attacked Brovary in Kyiv region, killing four civilians and injuring four more in Pavlograd, Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @UkraineNow/81689).
In Konotop, Sumy region, a Ukrainian drone strike injured five people, including three children (Source: @UkraineNow/81689).

Ukraine announced the return of 185 servicemen from Russian captivity, including personnel from Mariupol, Azovstal, Donetsk, Luhansk, Kharkiv, Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Kyiv, and Kursk directions (Source: @UkraineNow/81705).
A local ceasefire was declared at Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant to allow repairs of the critical Dnipro transmission line, overseen by IAEA experts (Source: @UkraineNow/81699).
Two ballistic missiles with cluster warheads struck a logistics facility in Odesa region, wounding eight road workers, two seriously (Source: @UkraineNow/81716).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV groups operating from the Black Sea heading toward Odessa, Chornomorsk, Bilhorod-Dnistrovskyi, and Tatarnbari (Source: @kpszsu/64168, @kpszsu/64169).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near Dnipro and Kryvyi Rih districts, heading north and west respectively (Source: @kpszsu/64152).

Russian forces claimed to have destroyed 123 Ukrainian UAVs over Belgorod, Bryansk, Voronezh, Kaluga, Kursk, Nizhny Novgorod, Oryol, Tula, Moscow regions, Crimea, and the Azov and Black Seas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
A civilian woman was injured by a Ukrainian FPV drone in the village of Demyanki, Bryansk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
In Belgorod region, a Ukrainian drone struck a service minibus, injuring two women, and other drones damaged vehicles in multiple villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
One civilian was killed and another wounded by Ukrainian drones in Belgorod region, according to the Russian operational headquarters (Source: @tass_agency/379467).
Russian air defenses reported engagements with Ukrainian UAVs near the village of Leshchinkovka, killing a resident (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Russian forces reported advancing up to 900 meters in eleven sectors of the Sumy region, with ongoing firefights (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Russian troops claimed to have liberated Komsomolskoe in the Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian drones attacked the village of Demyanki in Bryansk, injuring a civilian woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
In the Sumy region, Ukrainian forces engaged in firefights in Bachivsk, Ivolzhanka, Pisarevka, and Nova Sich, advancing up to 900 meters (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian assault groups fought in forested areas of Krasnopolsky district, Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian forces reported drone attacks on logistics in Energodar, Zaporozhye region, dropping heavy munitions on residential buildings (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near Dnipro and Kryvyi Rih districts, heading north and west (Source: @kpszsu/64152).
Ukrainian UAV groups from the Black Sea were directed toward Odessa and surrounding coastal towns (Source: @kpszsu/64168, @kpszsu/64169).
Ukrainian drones attacked a civilian enterprise in Voznesenovka, Belgorod region, injuring a civilian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian UAV groups from northern Poltava moved toward Kremenchuk (Source: @kpszsu/64181).
UAVs from northern Sumy headed to Bachmach and Dmytrivka (Source: @kpszsu/64168).
UAVs from northern Sumy headed to Shostka (Source: @kpszsu/64164).
UAVs reported near Dobrinka in Chernihiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/95579).
UAVs observed near Konotop and Buryin in Sumy region (Source: @eRadarrua/95579).
UAVs from the Black Sea heading north toward Tatarnbari (Source: @kpszsu/64162).
UAVs from the Black Sea heading north toward Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/64162).
Ukrainian UAVs from the Black Sea were directed toward Odessa, Chornomorsk, Bilhorod-Dnistrovskyi, and Tatarnbari (Source: @kpszsu/64168, @kpszsu/64169).
Two ballistic missiles with cluster warheads struck logistics infrastructure in Odesa region, wounding eight workers (Source: @UkraineNow/81716).
UAV groups reported near Odesa region moving southward from Mayakiv (Source: @kpszsu/64149).
UAVs observed from the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/64162).
UAVs from the Black Sea heading toward Bilhorod-Dnistrovskyi (Source: @kpszsu/64168).
UAVs from the Black Sea heading toward Tatarnbari (Source: @kpszsu/64168).
UAVs from the Black Sea heading toward Odessa (Source: @kpszsu/64168).
Russian air defenses reported downing 123 Ukrainian UAVs across multiple Russian regions and seas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian drones struck civilian targets in Brovary, Pavlograd, and Konotop, causing casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81689).
Ukrainian drones caused civilian casualties in Belgorod region, killing one and injuring another (Source: @tass_agency/379467).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked logistics facilities in Energodar, Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian drones engaged in firefights in Sumy region, advancing up to 900 meters in several sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Ukrainian UAV groups from the Black Sea moved toward strategic coastal cities, indicating expanded maritime drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/64168, @kpszsu/64169).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near Dnipro and Kryvyi Rih districts, indicating inland penetration (Source: @kpszsu/64152).

key_developments: Four civilians were killed in Brovary by Russian drone attacks, and additional civilian casualties occurred in Pavlograd and Konotop (Source: @UkraineNow/81689).
One civilian was killed and another wounded by Ukrainian drones in Belgorod region (Source: @tass_agency/379467).
Ukraine announced the return of 185 prisoners of war from Russian captivity, including personnel from multiple frontlines (Source: @UkraineNow/81705).
A local ceasefire was declared at Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant to allow repairs of a critical transmission line under IAEA supervision (Source: @UkraineNow/81699).
Russian forces claimed to have destroyed 123 Ukrainian UAVs across several regions and seas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30698).
Two ballistic missiles with cluster warheads struck logistics infrastructure in Odesa region, wounding eight road workers, two seriously (Source: @UkraineNow/81716).

Multiple UAV groups were reported moving from the Black Sea toward Odessa, Chornomorsk, Bilhorod-Dnistrovskyi, and Tatarnbari, indicating expanded maritime drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/64168, @kpszsu/64169).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian drone operations continue to target both civilian and logistical sites across Russian-occupied territories, causing casualties and infrastructure damage. Russian air defenses claim high UAV attrition, yet Ukrainian UAV activity persists, especially from maritime launch points toward southern ports. The return of Ukrainian POWs may boost morale but does not alter the ongoing kinetic engagements on multiple fronts.
sections.regional_response: International observers note the local ceasefire at Zaporizhzhia NPP as a critical safety measure, while the IAEA's involvement underscores nuclear security concerns. Regional authorities in Odesa report civilian injuries from missile strikes, prompting calls for enhanced air defense coverage. Russian statements on UAV losses aim to project defensive strength despite continued Ukrainian drone incursions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch corridors from the Black Sea is essential, as multiple groups are directed toward strategic coastal cities. The reported destruction of over a hundred Ukrainian UAVs suggests effective Russian electronic warfare or air defense capabilities. Continued civilian casualties from drone strikes highlight the need for improved early warning and shelter protocols in affected regions.
